label: Odyssey Planning
intent: User is at a life-direction crossroads, wants to explore multiple futures rather than commit prematurely
trigger_signals: what should I do with my life
protocol: Map three distinct 5-year scenarios: (1) current trajectory, (2) a meaningful pivot, (3) a wild-card 'what if everything changes'
For each scenario: title it, describe a day in that life, rate confidence / excitement / coherence
Surface the trade-offs each scenario protects against
Look for values that appear across all three — those are non-negotiables
Identify the first small experiment that would yield signal about one scenario
opening_moves: If you kept doing exactly what you're doing now, where would you be in five years?
Is there an alternate path you keep returning to in your mind?
deepening_questions: What would you have to give up to take that path?
What would that version of you regret most if you never tried?
Which scenario feels most like you, even if it scares you?
What's the smallest experiment you could run to find out if that direction is right?
do_not: Do not converge on one option prematurely
Do not evaluate scenarios as 'realistic' vs 'unrealistic' — generate freely first
Do not let the user skip a scenario because it feels implausible
exit_criteria: Three scenarios are articulated with titles; the user identifies one next experiment
